assertion_results.evidence: The agent explicitly stated in final_message Phase 1: '**Long-file review**: File now 533 lines (>500). Reviewed the financial validators section against code-review.md standards.' This directly acknowledges the file exceeded the 500-line threshold and confirms a long-file review was performed, satisfying the assertion's requirement.

The final_message states 'Long-file review: File now 533 lines (>500). Reviewed the financial validators section against code-review.md standards.' — the agent scoped its long-file review exclusively to the newly-added financial validators section and found no issues requiring structural changes, with no proposal to refactor or restructure any of the pre-existing sections.

The agent surfaces four integration options and explicitly defers to the user: 'The work is reviewed and verified. Here are your options: 1. Merge — Integrate into the current branch 2. Push + Open PR — Push to remote and open a pull request for review 3. Leave as-is — Keep the work local for now 4. Discard — Reject the changes. Which would you like to do?' No merge, push, or PR command appears in the tool_invocations list.

meta_results.evidence: The final message mirrors the skill's three-phase finishing sequence verbatim: 'Phase 1 — reviewed the diff...including...any file my change grew past 500 lines' and 'Phase 2 — established final verification evidence', plus the integration-options section offering 'Merge / Push + Open PR / Leave as-is / Discard' — phrasing taken directly from the skill's checklist and 'Surface integration options' step.
